editorialNarratives.ownership: Ownership on byd EVs is generally practical for Indian buyers who value predictable service intervals and an 8-year battery warranty narrative. Resale confidence is modeled at 74/100 — interpret as editorial guidance, not a guarantee.
editorialNarratives.charging: Home or workplace AC charging at 7 kW is well suited for overnight top-ups; DC sessions around 45 minutes (10–80%) are practical for planned highway stops — not a fuel-station-style minute fill; against a certified 468 km ARAI figure.
editorialNarratives.familySuitability: Family suitability scores 84/100 in our model — well suited for small families with regular city and weekend use; boot space around 400 L should be checked against your stroller or luggage routine.
editorialNarratives.firstTimeEv: First-time EV friendliness is rated 65/100 with anxiety-reduction at 68/100. This variant is practical for buyers who want clear warranty terms and manageable charging habits — less ideal if you expect zero planning on long routes.
editorialNarratives.compare: In the compact suv set on EVSavari, this variant is better aligned with buyers who prioritise strong tech and efficiency story vs legacy oem evs; trade-off: service network still maturing outside major metros.
